compiled_state.title: Onboarding Skill UX & Functionality Improvements
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
A non-technical user — such as a product manager or designer joining a Claude Code project for the first time — running /dso:onboarding encounters a disorienting experience: the process starts without explaining what it is or how long it will take, asks clinical true/false questions instead of natural dialogue, writes files without explaining their purpose, and fails with cryptic errors when macOS prerequisites (bash 4+, GNU coreutils) are missing. The Jira bridge is configured during onboarding but Figma and Confluence integrations are silently skipped, forcing users to discover and configure them manually later.

## Success Criteria
- At the start of onboarding, the skill displays an orientation message that states: this is a one-time project configuration, names the artifacts it will produce (project-understanding.md, dso-config.conf, shim), and shows a phase counter (Phase X of Y) that updates as each phase completes
- Before asking any questions, onboarding checks for required dependencies (bash >= 4.0 via /bin/bash --version, GNU coreutils, git) using Homebrew. If required deps are missing, it presents a single brew install command and pauses until the user confirms. Non-Homebrew platforms are out of scope — Homebrew is a documented prerequisite (see docs epic 91f5-0aec). Optional deps (ast-grep, semgrep) are offered separately and never block progress. Install commands include a timeout to prevent hanging in restricted network environments
- The preplanning interactive question uses natural-language phrasing and the answer is normalized to a boolean (true/false) before writing to dso-config.conf — ambiguous responses are clarified with a yes/no follow-up
- During the Phase 2 Socratic dialogue, onboarding asks about Jira, Figma, and Confluence integrations as optional configuration steps — each clearly labeled as skippable with a one-sentence explanation of what the integration enables. Credentials (API tokens, PATs) are NEVER written to dso-config.conf; only non-sensitive identifiers (project keys, space keys) are stored in config, with credentials referenced as environment variables
- Before writing each infrastructure file, the skill displays a 1-2 sentence plain-language explanation of what the file does and why it exists — covering at minimum: dso-config.conf (workflow settings), the .claude/scripts/dso shim (command-line shortcut to plugin scripts), and any pre-commit hooks (automated quality checks)
- A phase counter (Phase X of Y) is visible at the start of each major phase, where Y reflects the total number of phases defined in the skill's phase structure. When phases are skipped, the counter adjusts the total. Consider using the Task list tool to track progress through phases for built-in progress visibility
- Before/after validation on Nava template projects: (a) updated onboarding completes without errors on a fresh template project, (b) all three integration prompts (Jira, Figma, Confluence) appear in the dialogue, (c) the .claude/scripts/dso shim is present and executable after completion, (d) dso-config.conf contains no credential values, (e) phase counter displays at the start of each phase without gaps or jumps

## Scenario Analysis
- Bash 4+ detection fails on macOS split-brain (high): /bin/bash --version specified in SC2
- Figma token stored in config instead of env var (critical): SC4 explicitly prohibits credentials in dso-config.conf
- Integration skip path leaves ambiguous config state (medium): skipped integrations should write explicit disabled sentinel
- Re-run corrupts merge-not-overwrite for preplanning.interactive (high): must respect existing config
- Branch protections block tickets orphan branch push (high): existing push-failure warning
- CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT not set on first-time run (high): shim install depends on plugin system
- Natural-language question produces ambiguous boolean (medium): SC3 specifies normalization
- Optional dependency scan blocks in air-gapped environments (medium): SC2 specifies timeout

## Dependencies
Epic 91f5-0aec (onboarding documentation) handles static docs and Homebrew prerequisite documentation.

## Approach
Modify the /dso:onboarding SKILL.md to add a dependency pre-scan phase, rewrite clinical questions as natural dialogue, add integration setup steps for Figma/Confluence alongside existing Jira in Phase 2, add file-write explanations, and add progress tracking throughout.

comments.body:
VERIFICATION_RESULT: fail
criteria: 6/7 passed
SC2 (dependency pre-scan) fails: install commands for optional deps (ast-grep, semgrep) lack a timeout wrapper — SC requires 'install commands include a timeout to prevent hanging in restricted network environments' but Step 0 only implements a user-input default-to-N, not a process-level timeout on the brew/pip3 install invocations. Stories b267-a3b9 (SC1/SC6), fe2b-52dd (SC3), 0487-f12b (SC4), 4d15-157d (SC5), 12c8-a997 (SC7 structural), a979-a109 (SC7 docs) all verified PASS.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
VERIFICATION_RESULT: pass
criteria: 7/7 passed
All SCs verified PASS. SC2 re-verified after commit 5172c71e: timeout 120 wrappers confirmed on all executable optional dep install commands (pip3 install semgrep line 1259, brew install semgrep line 1261, brew install ast-grep semgrep Step 0 line 129).
